flashcards.front: What is a Tree in data structures?
flashcards.back: A hierarchical non-linear data structure consisting of nodes connected by edges, where each node has a parent-child relationship except the root node.

flashcards.front: What is the Root node in a tree?
flashcards.back: The topmost node of a tree that has no parent. It is the starting point from which all other nodes are reached.
A node with no children (degree zero). Also called an external node or terminal node.
A tree data structure where each node has at most two children, referred to as the left child and right child.

flashcards.front: What is a Complete Binary Tree?
flashcards.back: A binary tree in which all levels except possibly the last are completely filled, and nodes are as far left as possible.

flashcards.front: What is a Full (Proper) Binary Tree?
flashcards.back: A binary tree where every node has either 0 or 2 children. No node has exactly 1 child.

flashcards.front: Define the Height of a tree.
flashcards.back: The length of the longest path from the root to a leaf node. Root has height 0 in some definitions, or 1 in others.

flashcards.front: Define the Depth of a node.
flashcards.back: The length of the path from the root to that node. Root node has depth 0.
Visit left subtree, then root, then right subtree. For BST, produces sorted order.
Visit root first, then left subtree, then right subtree. Useful for creating copies of trees.
Visit left subtree, then right subtree, then root. Useful for deleting trees or evaluating expressions.
Breadth-first traversal where nodes are visited level by level from left to right using a queue.

flashcards.front: What is a Binary Search Tree (BST)?
flashcards.back: A binary tree where left child contains values less than parent, and right child contains values greater than parent.
A tree formed by selecting a node and all its descendants in a tree.

flashcards.front: Define Sibling nodes in a tree.
flashcards.back: Nodes that share the same parent. They are at the same level in the tree.
